channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever! — 41 replies — 164.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:34 UTC

OP

IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Early replies (first 3)

62283021
>>62283018
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283022
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283030
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283382
so why should we be immune from a Japan style 30 year stagnation? We had one before 1960. Allowing retards like Trump in power is begging for it.

62283411
>>62283382
Then buy Chinese or Euro stocks, most of which have already been sideways for ~2 decades.

62283417
>>62283382
Japan largely stopped being innovative desu, not America’s case so far

62283444
>>62283411
Many famous Euro stocks are actually down 30 or 40% since 2000 lol (Mercedes-Benz, Shell, Deutsche Bank, Philips, Bouygues, UniCredit, Veolia…)

62283452
>>62283185
So you need to have over 30m now to have what a millionaire had 1900. That's why millionaire was really special back then. So a million now is not even 30k in 1900.

62283465
>>62283444
I'm also amazed at some Chinese ones. Vanke used to be the flagship of Chinese stock market and the most prestigious real estate developer. The ticker number for it was chosen to be 000002, after 000001 being the Shanghai Composite Index

62283472
>>62283452
That distinction still exists, we call them UHNWIs nowadays

62283495
>>62283465
>Vanke
Looks like a crypto after the rug pull, almost as bad as Vivendi and Orange (the absolute juggernauts of the French stock market in 2000, they were supposed to crush the world with all their ambitious tech… 26 years later, still 90% down after the bubble burst…)

62283519
>>62283018
So they're just flat-out admitting that inflation has been 10% every year since 1950?

62283598
>>62283039
Is this inflation adjusted?

62283626
>>62283598
Dividing SPX by the USM2 is almost the same thing as adjusting to inflation.

62283790
>>62283411
How do you know which stock market might be the next Nikkei? It went up, until it didn't, and then took 35 years to recover

62283830
>>62283790
>which stock market might be the next Nikkei?
All of them

62284284
>>62283018
>just wait like 5 years goy, don't sell, it'll return to green eventually

62284502
>>62284284
>the twin towers of anon's portfolio

62284646
just buy and hold for 45 years, that's my motto

62284879
>>62284502
kek and they're right next to "september 11", I swear it was unintentional

62284961
>>62284646
>DUDE JUST WORK AND DEAL IN THE REAL WORLD FOR 45 YEARS

do goyim really?

62285025
>>62283021
>>62284284
>>62284646
whats the alternative?
>inb4 just buy gold

62286115
>>62284502
Kek

>>62285025
The market can keep on being exuberant for many months, so keep more and more cash by your side, a play that’s too contrarian like leveraged shorts could wipe you

62286525
>>62283018
>IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Until it doesn't.

62286553
>>62283258
>>Your current holdings get rekt by 90%
That never happened though. And you can just hodl and it usually bounces back within. A few months like OP's chart shows.

62286561
Wagmii

62286596
>>62283018
>IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Except for where it goes down

62286638
>>62286525
Literally the best time to buy though. How do you think gains are made.

62286688
>>62286638
Boughted at peak - fucked for 25 years

62286712
>>62286688
Wow you only ever got income once in 25 years and it corresponded to a market peak? What a wild life you've lived.

62286756
>Wow you only ever sold your house once in 25 years?
>Wow you only ever inherited once in 25 years?

62286769
>put brexit on the graph like england actually matters
holy delusional little broski wilted flower

62286806
>>62286688
Investing in 1935 looks comfy. 32 and 33 you had to perfectly time it to catch the bottom.

item.title: >turning 31 >just got a CDL I'm a loser and don't have many other options, but part of me is having cold feet because I… — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>turning 31 >just got a CDL I'm a loser and don't have many other options, but part of me is having cold feet because I…

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:46 UTC

OP

>turning 31
>just got a CDL
I'm a loser and don't have many other options, but part of me is having cold feet because I know that once I do this, the door to doing anything else basically closes because if I change my mind in five years I'll be nearly 40 and trying to start over again. I just want a remote job doing anything even if it's lower pay but not completely dead end; tried dabbling in coding and accounting but the threat of AI taking away the low value human capital positions made me lose a lot of motivation

Early replies (first 3)

62286399
>>62286379
>accountant
You need a very specific type of intelligence and worth ethic to be an accountant. lol.
You, a failure at 40 and trucking as an option, accounting definitely isnt for you.

62286405
>>62286379
I grew up in a very good area with one of the best school districts in the country in the first world.
I can 100% say I have a very good perspective on many things.
You need to be a certain type of person to be in accounting.
Hard to explain but if you know you know.
I grew up in a good area and from grade 1 to 12, I already knew the types that got straight A’s in class and exams and etc.

62286410
>>62286405
You don't need good grades rip be an accountant

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286477
>>62286458
You just don’t have a good grasp of anything. I was born very privileged so I know things. Good area and such.
If op has the option to choose between trucking and accounting at 31, he is definitely not the accounting type.
Its like being doctor, you don’t have to very intelligent but you really do need some certain traits. Not saying doctors are smart or that the profession isn’t tainted now, but you do need a certain personality to go in to it.
And yeah, that profession does tend to attract studious kids, students and people.

62286488
>>62286458
It’s like saying an athletic good looking popular person is less likely to study accounting or be a doctor.
An athletic and person who loves movement is going to have a hard time sitting still and studying and reading 7hours a day for years.
Your personality kinda pushes you into a specific career.
You also need to be smart enough to ignore some instincts.
Like in my early 20s, I was into bodybuilding so I chose a part time physical job, turns out most people don’t have opportunities like me and people don’t really get to join and quit as many jobs as me.
So most physical jobs are worked by low iq barely human people. People that operate on emotions and instincts like animals.

62286492
>>62286379
>got cdl
Didn’t this already require a ton of time and $ already?

62286519
>>62286379
great men have successfully changed careers in their 50s, don't be a bitch. Get the bag and invest it into tech, bitcoin, and gold, retire by 48, do whatever u want.

62286557
>>62286519
These are great men and they are a handful.
For the majority, personality and characteristics dictate what people choose as a career path.

62286573
>>62286519
Wtf is invest, bro?
99% of people don’t even have 1m invested.
“Investing” is literally for the rich.

62286579
>>62286379
I’m not sure what to tell you OP but I’m sorry your thread got hijacked by that fag.

62286583
>>62286461
You have made a bunch of grammar errors so I'm just going to assume you're ESL

62286587
>>62286579
Pretty sure he's the guy who's always posting about "high status" or whatever, he spams every thread he enters

62286605
>>62286443
>first entry level role
I guess I should just apply to these but I live in a small town and there's only a handful. They all want an associates and/or years of experience just to make $20/hr. If I even got the interview I'd probably fail it because I have no relevant skills but maybe I should do it now, I have nothing to lose

62286615
>>62286583
you're likely conversing with a jeet

62286618
>>62286379
Buy chainlink you dumbfuck

62286620
>>62286405
You will never have sex as an accountant. And if you do it will be way too awkward for any chick to enjoy unless she has cerebral palsy.

62286621
>>62286379
i started over when i was 40

62286636
Don't listen to the kvetching anon larping as an accountant, you could do trucking while studying in your free time to be an accountant.

62286644
>>62286620
>Muh dick
>>62286615
>Muh jeet

62286645
You earned a CDL.

You can work towards something else.

Just don't stop and never be a lazy piece of shit.

You;ll be good.You realize you typed this shit on $skins right. Good luck yo!

62286668
ITT
Anons who don’t want to admit characteristics and traits dictate what profession you go into.
Just like how blue collar wagies don’t want to admit they are barely 70 iq monkeys.
Go walk into a construction site in your shithole town, every worker there looks like a golem.

62286690
>>62286668
ITT anon tries to twist accounting from the paperwork equivalent of Sisyphus where gay ass guidelines are mounted more and more and one little fuckup blows up the whole mountain to "no but seriously these redditors are the best and the brightest, you have to get all of the best medals at star academy".
I switched to statistics for a reason, accounting is the janitor of banking.
>>62286379
Don't go into accounting, you'll shoot up the place within a month (the school or whatever poor takes you as an intern), also they're replacing the entry level shit with pajeets, especially the big 4.

62286691
>>62286644
Yea muh dick. Lot lizards will blow him while you’ve never had a girl touch your dick

62286710
>>62286691
Kinda hoping he is better than that. But if that's his this thing, why not?

Don;t do that Anon.

62286748
>>62286379
i do feel sorry for you because trucking can be demanding and driving is dangerous, but at the same time i've heard of truckers living great lives so i don't feel bad for you. you should have gotten a bachelors in something if you didnt want a working job.

62286759
>>62286748
So this example is from the boomer period but I remember one of my dad's friends going into trucking instead of using his engineering degree because he simply could not take the stress of living in a major metro. Like his life wasn't all sunshine and rainbows but he made do. Get a truck fridge full of reta and chicken breast, you won't get too fat.

62286761
Truckers deliver all the stuff we want to buy.

Hang in there. Drive safe yo!

Stay alert and take it seriously.

62286783
>>62286477

This is the most autistic board.

62286785
Lol this thread is a good reflection of society.
No one wants to admit they are low status even though their job is exactly that. low status.

62286786
>>62286690
>accounting is the janitor of banking
kek

62286826
I agree with the personality guy. Most jobs require other personality traits than just being smart to do well. Really want to find something that is a good fit for your brain and environment if possible or you are going to have a bad time.

62286828
>>62286785
OP started his post by saying he is a loser. That set up this thread to be that way. In the future stop posting like a loser and fag. No offense OP. People need to stop being afraid to post proud. If you want to be a trucker, just say I'm a trucker! Then maybe talk shit about people who aren't truckers. Let's have fun and not be pussys.

Start acting like men! Tell some bullshit story about fucking people's mom"s who are lot lizards. Just kiding, be respectful. Stand up and take pride in your work.

62286834
>>62286618
I can stop being an accountant whenever this dogshit actually pumps
>>62286605
Yea getting that first experience is the hardest part but it sets you up well to get a good job afterwards

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 53 replies — 96.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:15 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275142
>>62273654
Sort of like how your dad was replaced by my penis

62275176
>>62272812
Tits or ass

62275435
>>62272812

Are you going to be an actual trial attorney with balls or be a paper pushing cuck like most?

I can't make up my mind who's intelligence the general public overrates more between doctors and lawyers.

62275484
>>62272812
What was your LSAT score? What is your school’s rank? What was your GPA? Where are you interning this summer?

How big is your pp?

62275816
>>62275176
Ass for most of my life but as I’ve gotten older, tits
>>62275435
I honestly don’t know yet, it’s only been one year and I’m not even dead set on which area of the law I want to practice in.
>>62275484
>What was your LSAT score?
170
>What is your school’s rank?
Mid 20s
>What was your GPA?
In undergrad or 1L? In undergrad it was a 3.4 in an easy major, I just didn’t give a shit in college. In 1L it was a 3.5
>Where are you interning this summer?
I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

>How big is your pp?
5.8 inches massive

62275842
Do you ever consider yourself a second class profession compared to finance chads who value and trade and make money off the silly little financial obligation contracts lawyers write up?

62275952
>>62275842
Again dude I just finished my first out of three years of law school, so I don’t know what the actual practice looks like at all.

62276043
>>62275952
I'm just ribbing you buddy, a little white collar banter if you will

62277022
>>62275816
>170 LSAT

Acceptable.

>Mid 20s Law School

Acceptable.

>3.5 1L GPA

Acceptable.

>I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

It’s over. You are cooked. BIG oof. Thanks for playing.

62277039
>>62277022
Why is it over? And why didn’t you address my penis size?

62277080
>>62277039
If you are not interning at an AMLAW 50 firm your 1L summer then you literally need to kill yourself.

[spoiler]My posts are intended to satirize the rampant one-upsmanship in the legal profession. I don’t actually care about your genitalia you dumb retard. It is a metaphor.[/spoiler]

62278084
>>62277080
Well don’t scare me like that anon. You freaked me out since some of the people in my class do have Big Law internships and it’s kinda tough not to feel jealous sometimes with how much they’re getting paid.

62278202
>>62278084
Funposting aside, (You) should be (mildly) freaked out because spending your 1L summer split between volunteering for a professor and a one (1) month stint with a (non-federal) jjudge is concerning. Especially if the stats (You) quoted me earlier are true.

(You) are underachieving.

62278255
>>62278202
I don't know what to tell you. I applied for a billion 1L summer positions and even got a lot of interviews but none of them panned out. It would have been nice to get something more prestigious looking but I'm just glad I finally got something lined up. i have heard also that as long as you do do something legal related 1L summer then firms don't really care what it was, is that not true?

I'm not actually aiming for Big Law or anything, I'm mostly interested in environmental and real estate law and potentially being a prosecutor. I dunno I've only taken the basic classes and it feels weird this expectation to know for certain what you want to do before you've learned anything about the field.

62278315
>>62278255
I appreciate your sincerity, and I will stop frogposting.

Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid. I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms. These days, Big Law internships are reserved for black lesbians (unironically). You’re doing fine.

Godspeed.

62278366
>>62278315
>Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid
I am getting paid for the RA job though.
>I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms
This might be self-evident as a 4chan poster but I absolutely hate networking, really suck at it, and would like to avoid it as much as possible. Is it really that essential to getting a good job? Do I just have to force myself to do it? I heard the 2L summer hiring process was supposed to be way chiller and less insane.

And thanks for your advice by the way!

62278395
>>62278366
Networking is somewhat required for any career path unfortunately. My condolences anon

62280144
>>62278395

62280365
>>62278366
You will miss out of some of the best opportunities in this profession if you don’t network, unfortunately. I only got my current job because one of my professors was willing to refer me to someone on the inside.

62280758
Are there any good legal loopholes for evading debt collection? I'm writing a story, and there's a chapter where the MC scrambles to hide his assets from the chapter 7 trustee.

62281712
>>62280758
I know several but will tell you none

62282639
>>62280758
>I'm writing a story
I dunno if I believe you there anon

62286326
>>62272812
Truebit was hacked in January you think a lawsuit has a chance?

62286469
>>62272812
How much are jews over-represented in law?

Can I get in with a science degree and mediocre GPA (3.5)? If i can't get a science job with my MS then thinking of pivoting into patent law.

62286482
what are the reputations of people who go into different types of law? like are torts people regarded as ambulance chasers? people who go into real estate considered retards? etc

62286539
how do I sue companies for discrimination during the interview process?

62286591
>>62273041
>the irish people have done nothing for humanity, and neither will you. You are niggers compared to Russians

62286708
>>62273090
What were your classmates like? Like how nursing is single moms and programming is white sexual deviants and engineering students are just a bunch of Aryan chads.

62286727
>>62272812
Kys lawfag

62286755
>>62286326
Probably not
>>62286469
Definitely overrepresented but I dunno how much.
>>62286482
I definitely know personal injury lawyers are seen as being kind of skeavy, the civil rights and public interest stereotype is they're very wealthy and privileged with their parents paying for everything but still act condescending towards everyone trying to get paid. I don't really know stereotypes for the rest.
>>62286539
You basically can't unless you have a smoking gun like emails between each other saying they were discriminating or they accidentally sent you a voicemail joking about that guy they just discriminated against
>>62286708
Probably 60% women, and 95% white other than one asian girl. There were a few different cliques but the only really shared trait was alcoholism and being nerds. Some are the friendly type of nerds and some the smug type of nerds. Like there was one guy who would go to TA office hours and when someone would ask the TA a question he would very quickly butt in and try to answer the question for the TA, then at the end ask the TA "is that basically right?" pissed people off. Most people were just normal though.
>>62286727
Why come?

item.title: This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle. — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle.

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:41 UTC

OP

This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle.

Early replies (first 3)

62285843
>eth/sol/bnb
I hope niggy 10xes from here. Not because it would make me rich, but because of all the other shit around it that would pump.

62285851
>>62285810
A non-LINK project? On the LINK board?
They use their own oracles as well?
AND it's only gone up since?

No no no this will not do. Jannies send this nigger to the shadow realm

62285856
>>62285810
buy a fucking ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285858
>>62285856
Kek there he is

62285933
>>62285856
you've fed sergey enough big macs dont you think? time to give it a rest

62285953
>>62285810
>edgex enters the chat

62285995
>>62285810
Some anon pointed out that it feels like a new meta for alts. Kind of had to retruth that

62286128
>>62285995
it's been clear as day this has been the case for a while now as long as you weren't afflicted with bag bias from holding some of these absolutely raped alts from previous cycles with a team/VC unlock schedule that looks like a crash game.
It just sucks that it took a perp dex to finally align itself fully with its baggies but oh well.

Trump also made a Truth post yesterday explicitly mentioning
>Crypto Perpetuals
>Offshore company
>Bringing them back to the US
And guess which company fits all 3. I'm actually cautiously optimistic this orange fucker maybe kingmakes Hyperliquid but realistically he's probably going to do some scummy shit with WLFI to extract even more from his goyim base

62286151
>>62286128
I hope Trump doesn't touch HYPE. Everything that orange faggot and his family touches turns to ash. I don't want to be an employim anymore

62286180
ah there it is just confirms it.

62286206
>>62286151
He's minted ungodly gains for his stock picks like Intel, Micron, and Dell today but his crypto shills have only nuked. In the event he does shill I hope for both our sakes its the former and a genuine shill

62286218
>>62286151
cheeto mussolini is mostly interested in making money for himself and his friends. In crypto this means shilling some shit that pumps aand then rugs. In stocks it means number only go up.

62286304
>>62286206
OK fair enough. 100 sui 1000 make it we thinking?

62286343
>>62286304
I think 10,000 would be a decent make it stack. There's still a fat chunk of supply that is held under rewards, whether its a 2nd airdrop or staking rewards, no clue

62286514
>>62285810
hype, pump and tao are the big 3. this is a hype, pump and tao board now.

62286671
The nano second the Glamsterdam update releases there will be 3 hyperliquid competitors launching, with instant access to $40 Billion in liquidity on L1 (Hype only have $2 billion). JEFF WE RUE THE DAY HE BETRAYED VITALIK BY LAUNCHING A SPERATE EVM L1.

62286723
This just happened
Jeffrey Sprecher, CEO of ICE (Owners of the NYSE)

62286733
>>62286723
Yeah, it's happening

62286743
>>62286723
>>62286733
Bullish

62286801
>>62285810
60B fdv....

62286820
>>62286801
so what, it isnt random vc niggers getting the unlocks, it's the team and they've shown they dont turbo dump like sirgay and his band of faggots

item.title: If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY? — 17 replies — 49.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:27 UTC

OP

If I invest $10k in this, how much will I have by EOY?

Early replies (first 3)

62286606
10k

62286609
8k

62286611
>>62286602
$10k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286614
>>62286602
$7500

62286617
>>62286602
2K

Maybe I think it would go private beiefly though so you may just get bought out for 2$ a share before it goes private again elon ah heroes and it's banrupted or bought by boeing for a dollar

62286623
And ignore emotional investing advice like >>62286617 btw

62286627
>>62286602
>▶▶
>>▶
It's not a scam, but I'd be very interested to see an IPO that didn't tank after the first few days.

62286630
>>62286623
UNAUDITED
>>>62286623
>emotional investing
YEAH?

62286634
>>62286630
Yeah

62286641
>>62286627
Coinbase hasn't even recovered to it's ipo kek

62286642
>>62286602
I bought Velo 3D, because they make spacex’s 3d printers. It’s been doing great.

62286651
>>62286602
6k

62286658
WELCOME TO THE GRAND COMMUNIST CIRCUS
Step right up, comrades! In today’s performance, everybody owns everything… which somehow means nobody can find toilet paper. HONK HONK.
First act: The government confiscates the bakery to “help the people.” Five minutes later the shelves looking like they got raided by raccoons. But don’t worry! A man in a giant red suit appears yelling, “Production has increased by 9000%!” while standing directly in front of an empty store. Audience applauds nervously.
Second act: The Amazing Disappearing Freedom Trick! One second you say, “Hey maybe this system ain’t working,” and POOF — now you taking an unscheduled vacation to a mysterious work camp somewhere cold enough to freeze soup solid. Incredible! Absolute cinema.
Third act: Equality for all! Everybody gets the same tiny apartment, the same sad bread loaf, and the same mandatory portrait of Supreme Mustache Leader smiling at you from every wall like a haunted mascot. Meanwhile the party elites eating steak backstage talking about “shared sacrifice.” HONK HONK HONK.
And the economy? Brother, the economy riding a unicycle on fire juggling potatoes. Every problem gets solved with another five-year plan written by twelve dudes who never touched a shovel in their life.
Communism really be like:“We fixed greed!”creates a bigger greedy government
Peak clown technology.

62286735
>>62286658
Dood elon musk is raising cows in space, spacecows!

62286776
>>62286602
$1,000,000

62286779
>>62286602
10k minus broker fees

62286790
>>62286735
Yo, SpaceX out here moving like that one dude in class who borrows money for lunch then flexes fake chains on Instagram. They screaming “future of humanity” while burning billions like it’s a mixtape launch party. Bro got rockets exploding on livestream so often people think it’s a Netflix trailer.

Meanwhile the finance students gotta calculate ROI, cash flow, and risk management while SpaceX be treating investor money like GTA cheat codes. “Yeah bro, we lost another rocket… but next one gonna be fire.” Literally.

Their whole business model sounds like a group project carried by government contracts. NASA basically the sugar daddy keeping the lights on while Elon tweets memes at 3 AM like he’s avoiding accounting homework. Imagine telling your professor: “My company valuation is vibes and Mars dreams.” You’d get escorted out the lecture hall instantly.

And don’t even start on the Mars talk. My guy can’t get reliable Wi-Fi on Earth but wants people paying rent on another planet. Finance professors teaching discounted cash flow while SpaceX valuation is discounted reality flow. Every launch got people acting like it’s the second coming of Einstein, then boom — another stainless steel bottle rocket doing cartwheels over Texas.

Street truth? SpaceX is that rich kid who crashes daddy’s Lambo, buys another one, and somehow gets called a genius entrepreneur. In any normal finance case study, half these decisions would be labeled “high-risk speculative behavior with catastrophic downside exposure.” But slap a cool rocket edit on TikTok and suddenly everybody forgets basic economics.

At this point the only thing reaching orbit consistently is the hype.

item.title: how are there so many women with good paying white collar jobs? — 13 replies — 20.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
how are there so many women with good paying white collar jobs?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:10 UTC

OP

how are there so many women with good paying white collar jobs? what are these women doing that we men arent understanding?

Also there are so many executive women that are like 25.. I open dating apps in a big city and every 25 year old girl is "head of sales" "VP of marketing" "director of HR" and so on

how????

Early replies (first 3)

62286484
If you’re 25 and unmarried as a woman you failed at life, kek

62286486
>>62286475
They flirt with horny executives. If you talk with them, you'll realize how unqualified they are.

62286494
>be me
>have years of practical experience working in a particular industry as a contractor because I was told simply having a degree wasn't good enough to be hired to a full time corporate job
>interviewed by a female supervisor
>she's 10 years younger than me and was promoted to supervisor after only 1 year working there
>I'm applying for the open job she left
>didn't get the job
Why is it not legal to do a massive life ruining lawsuit against these roasties

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286511
>>62286475
I will explain simply OP.

Women are built for doing well in school and the corporate system due to their increased submissiveness making them ideal wage slaves that can then be promoted into managing the new wage slaves as they come up. The whole system re-architecting is now complete such that women excel above men in all high status areas, with a few exceptions. The data on women vs. men 20-35 is clear. On top of that, they are given preference by beta males and queen bees in hiring panels.

62286560
women are good at doing work as portrayed in movies or tv, which means having meetings. women like meeting with each other. they all have the dumb, unnecessary desire for building consensus before anything is decided upon. more than once I’ve had a woman put a meeting on my calendar to ask me about color choices for an internal, non-customer facing tool we use.

62286582
>>62286475
yea

62286586
Dude how can you not knowing something existed for thousands of years, housewives has always been able to afford luxury, fast car, fast house, jewellery, gold chains rings etc etc. no free lecture for numb skulls

62286678
>>62286475
HR... They only hire wimmins and browns now.

62286707
>>62286586
Ahhhh, yup executive position is a luxury just like anything else woman possesses in the past. I think would expect you to be humble to the ground to accept an entry level janitor position.

62286716
>>62286475
they get to abuse nepotism and cry victim when called out

62286734
>>62286475
As I see it, women didn’t used to be hired for these roles because they were genuinely not qualified. Now that they’re getting educated - even at slightly higher rates than men - employers salivate at the opportunity to hire a woman because they get white knight points, and they know that women aren’t as competitive as men so they make good wagies even if they can be annoying and are not quite as qualified as their male counterparts. Plus bonus points for having boobies

62286738
>>62286475
women love being around people because its way harder to rape them when theres a lot of people around

62286746
I think they were just good at being social at the job fairs they host for everyone coming out of college. If i was a recruiter I would be hiring bimbos for all my entry level positions.

item.title: Why did CLL pivot from staking to the LINK reserve? — 12 replies — 18.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why did CLL pivot from staking to the LINK reserve?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:08 UTC

OP

Did anyone else notice how CLL silently pivoted from their original vision of staking collateral to buybacks (the LINK reserve), which means that revenue instead of going to stakers is getting monopolized by CLL and then they decide in a centralized opaque way how much of that revenue goes into buybacks.

Early replies (first 3)

62285813
>>62285776
ever notice how youre a faggot? link is dead, pal

62285824
>>62285776
go read the clarity act fuck nut

62285846
>>62285824
So how will you cope when clarity act gets passed and there's still no real staking?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285909
>stakers not pictured below

62285915
>>62285846
my life doesn't revolve around chainlink tokenomics. I already pulled out my initial plus profit. If staking never happens I've already won. If it does then I make it. simple as

62285922
>>62285776
>You ever notice how ever since we stopped getting surprised by peekaboo, mom has started jingling keys in front of us instead?

62285954
>>62285915
grim

62285960
>>62285776
Me on the left. I literally look like that

62285971
>>62285776
>original vision of staking collateral
that was an invention of this retarded board. staking was imagined by CLL as a mechanism for decentralizing nodes, enforcing good behavior through slashing. but this was never realized because it's impossible to implement for pretty much every service chainlink provides

62285972
>>62285954
>grim
says the dude seething 6 years past the top? Oof. Couldn't be me

62285979
>>62285971
>says that staking collateral was an invention of this board
>proceed to explain exactly the same thing
ok
>but this was never realized because it's impossible to implement for pretty much every service chainlink provides
it's so fucking over, whatever happened to us?
so we've just collectively given up on real staking??????
And we are supposed to be ok with the retarded LINK buybacks thing?

62286737
>>62285915
>>62285972
this is called cope, stinkie
keep on copin'
cuz you ain't makin' it off stinks

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:09 UTC

62282404

NIG NIGGER
NIG NIGGER
NIGGER NIGGER NIG GUH GUH

62282417
>>62282404

item.title: Chinese tech stocks — 8 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chinese tech stocks

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-29 00:46 UTC

OP

These last couple of years has been pretty generational for American tech stocks. Anything addressing the AI rollout has pretty much gone vertical. Mag7 is at ath, MU, SNDK, etc. all up 10-20x in a year. And there is plenty more. This is on the back of massive private and public AI spending (7.8tril by 2028 or whatever). These were great opportunities and could very well continue to go up.

I've started looking at other countries. KOSPI up almost 200% in a year, NIKKEI almost 70% in a year. However I have noticed Chinas market is pretty much flat/slightly down. I know China is dealing with various issues (stagflation, unemployment, low productivity, gov intervention etc.), but this feels like a massive discrepancy.

I'm gathering a list of some solid Chinese tech stocks as I think they have the most room to grow. In particular I'm liking BABA, BAIDU, CQQQ and XIOAMI. These companies are all exposed to AI growth, cloud, compute and data centre rollout across China and the world. If these companies were in the USA they would automatically be trading close to 10x they are now just cause thats the norm.

I am also looking at Vietnamese tech stocks too but thats more niche. I think the US market continues to do well and thats fine, but the bulk of the money has been made. Chinese tech stocks is my prediction and I would love some input as to why these aren't a good buy.

Early replies (first 3)

62286501
>>62286463
China doesn’t want people investing into their market. Simple as that.
They don’t want funny money shit.
China values stability. You can invest into their blue chips I guess.

62286558
>>62286501
Stability will mean China loses the AI race and enriches their biggest rival. I understand it may have been that way, but at some point they need to compete. The companies are solid, revenue and infrastructure. It almost feels like at some point the blue chips I mentioned will need to pump to compete on the stage with USA.

62286564
>>62286463
If I said the American stock market was a casino, a joke, a farce or a circus, you would know exactly what I mean. (Even if it isn’t 100% true obviously)
The Chinese and their culture don’t want that for their stock maket.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286572
>>62286558
>loses the AI race
there is no race. the AI mania in the west is equivalent to china spending billions on empty uninhabited ghost cities. crony capitalism

62286595
>>62286564
I value your input, I just feel like at some point the difference of 300b for BABA and 3t for AMZN will eventually even itself out, and the Chinese counterparts stand to gain the most in this situation. Just an observation.

>>62286572
AI has revenue to back it up. And China will always need their own versions of tech so they don't overly rely on the west. Sure its crony capitalism but the $$ being transferred between different companies is ridiculous (AI -> hardware -> memory -> AI) and surely China has to engage or be left victims to the empire of the USA.

62286600
>>62286558
>>62286572
Yeah. There isn’t a “race.”
China also has cheap energy apparently.
Also, the fact Bassent and Trump literally go on tv everyday and say
>China is far behind the usa
means China probably isn’t behind or far behind. Even Jensen Huang said “China isn’t behind” and then immediately had to correct what he said.
Since China has cheap emergy, they can just use more chips or whatever. Jensen did a segment on this.

62286604
>>62286595
> surely China has to engage or be left victims to the empire of the USA.
Go watch some Jensen Huang interviews. I am sure he knows more than us. He doesn’t say China is behind.

62286660
>>62286604
I will thank you. But in a way does that not also mean these companies are undervalued? pic rel seems like they are a combination of google and amazon. Or maybe I am just looking at it through the lens of Americanisation.

item.title: Rescue your dying value — 8 replies — 5.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Rescue your dying value

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-29 00:20 UTC

OP

Swap your junk for XMR, purposeless coins all going to 0 before Jan 1 2028

Early replies (first 3)

62280927
>>62280863
yeah im good.

i like XMR but the community is giga cringe anytime you mention anything about price.

ETH is going to valhalla

62281189
>>62280927
absolute knuckle dragging retard right here

62281362
>>62280863
WHAT IS THIS KUMAR SHILL KEK? Its okay form a fucking shitnigger icp or sol holder, but u pedofags too? u guys r cheap nowadays...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62281860
>>62280927
>i like XMR but the community is giga cringe anytime you cry about not making gorillions off the delisted DNM money
ftfy

62284304
>>62280863
That's actually really good advice.

62285354
Cheapies for slurp right now. XMR will never be this cheap ever again. Get yours now before it pumps 20% tomorrow

62286570
BUYING OPPORTUNITY

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 52 replies — 62.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-05-29 00:57 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285389
>>62285363
ok, im reading about it, surely you understand im skeptic, right? 1% sl, 80% volume reduction and 10x leverage i would have made 1B
what is trading volatility? derisking by how much?
what do you recommend in terms of setting (sl for shorts longs the same?)
if you make me money, i'll gladly give you 5-10% of my profits

62285426
>>62285389
>1% sl, 80% volume reduction and 10x leverage i would have made 1B
I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
>what is trading volatility? derisking by how much?
up to your own style. Like I said, the demo strategy just sells 50% on Volatility confirmation, since the goal of that strategy is just to show how an almost-passive investor can make gains with 4-6 trades a month
One user scalps ETH liquidation levels using it as a bull/bear bias and says he's printing
>if you make me money, i'll gladly give you 5-10% of my profits
no need anon, I'm not charging anyone money until I've got the model tuned into ridiculous accuracy. maybe give the twitter a follow and turn notes on, leave a like or comment on the posts, that'd be a big help

62285441
>>62285363
Ah I see. But if you sell at red and buy at green you shouldn't be holding any at yellow anyways. So derisk should only really happen if you're shorting.

Anyways my experience with short term trading crypto is you get eaten alive by the fees. Not only are the fees exorbitantly higher than for stocks but you get charged per dollar not per coin/share. For example on kraken you have to be doing over a million in monthly volume just to get your fees down to a reasonable rate and even thats assuming you never make market orders.

62285468
You guys still use meme indicators? I simply look at the moon

62285477
>>62285441
>But if you sell at red and buy at green you shouldn't be holding any at yellow anyways. So derisk should only really happen if you're shorting.
The Volatility signals are weird inflection points where you can get either early trend reversals, or sudden trend accelerations - combining it with the FX layer is really good at identifying which of those it's likely to be, and confirming/invalidating the signal.
For example,
>>62285172
Here you got a Buy followed by a Volatility with FX counter-trend to price trend (negative) - the implication is exactly what happened, a short-term bullish impulse
>>62284814
The May 25 Sell was followed by the 26th Volatility with inline FX to price trend (negative) - implies acceleration to the downside, and man did that happen

62285480
>>62285468
Why look at the moon when you can look at the wave anon

62285577
how do i find this? website?

62285614
>>62285577
Watermark

62285615
>>62285426
>I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
yeah, most likely. Definitely tune it, it's super useful to understand how tight should the strategy be.

explain me something, i'm seeing two future signals (may 29 and may 30), this won't change if i understand correctly, that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website? but the confirmation layer will only be updated in that day, yes? I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
Finally when are the trades done? Immediately when a new daily candle starts? Or is there any rule?

i'm going to give you a follow for sure

62285636
>>62285615
>that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website?
First one that confirms. If FX continues this positive tomorrow, 2h after US open it'll confirm. Honestly I think buys today are good but that's not what the forecast back on the 3rd said
> I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
The FX gauges are the confirmation layer, Strategy is an oscillator using several weighted factors (physics predictions, FX on different timeframes, seasonality, etc) that's supposed to give you a simple, accurate bias for the day. It's all WIP but in its defense it was screaming Strong Sell on the 25th and man was that accurate
>Finally when are the trades done?
They execute immediately on confirmation, right now I'm working on a fully automated bot that just uses Strategy and a basic Bollinger strat for entries/exits
>i'm going to give you a follow for sure
thanks anon! Likes and comments on the posts are maybe the biggest possible help right now

62285706
>>62285636
is there anything i can read about the schizo psychics this is based on?

62285733
>>62285706
From the other thread, Econophysics as a concept has been around since Bacherie's "Theorie de la speculation" in 1900, but what I'm doing is based on "Price variations in a stock market with many agents" from 1997 as a foundation, and "Financial brownian particle in the layered order-book fluid and fluctuation-dissipation relations" from 2014, and then applying a pretty simple schizo concept to take the reasoning one step further. The end result is predictive weeks in advance

62285770
>>62285733
Gonna look into it
tks anon

62285791
>>62285480
The moon creates the tides

62285803
>>62285791
>wild shrimp need tides to reproduce
>moon creates tides
>moon makes shrimp sandwiches possible
Thanks moon

62285830
>>62285103
>https://shiyu-coder.github.io/Kronos-demo/
this shit looks overfit nigga id backtest it at least

62285839
I have a similar model coded up in pine it is quite useless lol

62285878
>>62285839
lmao this one works a little better, my main problem was underestimating the violence we got on fairly faint signals this last 3 days, which might be partially the nature of the simulation - outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Still, calling every major pivot this month using schizo math is pretty decent

62285897
>>62285878
shut up bot

62285919
>>62285897
no u

62285940
>>62285919
you probably work for the cia retard

62285967
>>62285940
>CIA
>giving away the free money cheat code to internet autists
doesn't seem very likely anon

62286025
>>62285967
I mean as long as this doesn't go full premium (as a ploy to ensnare gullible /biz/ tards) it seems like a good resource. Some ppl on this board want to stay poor or just FUD for fun. Pathetic behavior honestly.

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62286532
>>62284814
>buy may 17
>sell for a loss on May 25

thanks

62286552
>>62286532
>buy may 17
>short may 25
you're welcome

62286639
>>62286447
>because it's a lot easier
Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
>I'm a nerd, not a trader
So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.

I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.

62286700
>>62286639
>Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
A lot of it is! I keep getting tempted to outsource some of it to an LLM but some gut instinct says "you'd be really really dumb to do that," even a local one
>So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.
Oh, I use it, have been trading perps with it for years, getting more profitable as the model evolves. After the addition of the FX layer last spring, I can get away with some truly disgusting leverage. I just meant I don't have a background in trading or technical analysis, so I've been learning all of those skills on the go. It's all related but still a different skillset
>I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.
Please do, I'll post in here when there's updates but you can always get in touch via Twitter

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 118 replies — 40.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 118 • Updated: 2026-05-28 22:53 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278858
>>62273985
It had always been fishy.

62280044
>>62278837
You can literally verify everything yourself. We’re way past the point where major players like Aave, Ledger, GoMining, a16z and others would touch obvious garbage for no reason.

62280052
>>62272017
A lot of people still view the market through old cycle assumptions.

62280441
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a liquidity sink and will be rugged in a bug deflatinary swoop when the time is right.

62280858
>>62273653
problem with index funds is you just never make enough to make it unless you're like 20-30 years at it

62282426
>>62280858
cryoto is better than everything

62282600
>>62276888
you would've sold them at $10

62282845
>>62271712
I just inherited almost a million usd and I'd like to put 100,000 into bitcoin but I don't know how :(

I'm also very concerned about getting ripped off :(

I also don't want to have to deal with taxes :(

Will you help me? Is there a way to buy it and somehow store it offline or is it only accessible online? Do I have to make some account at Binance or some other site?

62282847
>>62271712

62282849
>>62280044
There are many similar offerings ended up nothing so it's scary

62282854
>>62282845
Send it to me

62282864
>>62282845
kys
see
>>62282847

62282885
>>62282864
I don't wanna kill myself though. I just became a millionaire! Life is finally getting good for me :)

I just wanna buy a bitcoin. Why won't you help me?

62283041
>>62272050
Hmmm.. what do you mean?

62283050
>>62282849
That’s why people should wait for the actual TBV launch first instead of screaming revolution or scam this early.
At least the Aave v4 governance process is already moving now.

62283055
>>62272643
At least check it sometimes bro. You might already be retired and just don’t know it yet.

62283065
>>62272208
A 2x or 3x from here would still outperform most traditional investments by a huge margin.

62283375
>>62283050
Defi hacks are no joke

62283377
>>62282845
Why are you flexing your imagination?

62283380
>>62271712
Money

62283769
>>62276880
ARMA hasn't passed yet, but there's already a Reserve.

62283901
THE BIG DUMPING
>every cycle 90-70% loss. why would it be this time different.

62283926
>>62283901
diminishing returns mean diminishing drawbacks, retard

62283940
>>62282847
I hope that big brain stuck around, for my sake

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

62285517
>>62272225
They've got their preferences

62285578
>>62283375
Yeah, DeFi hacks are getting pretty crazy lately. That’s why multiple audits, formal verification, penetration testing, and cryptography reviews matter much more now with this much capital involved.

62285585
>>62272647
You'll regret your choices lol

62285589
>>62272667
You think you're smart?

62286335
>>62271712
No money, no job
They literally won't even take me at minimum wage places because I don't speak spanish.
So, no way to generate the income to buy BTC in the first place.

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 18 replies — 33.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-29 01:16 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285966
>>62285713
TP? Where are you? And do you still own BAT?

62286043
>>62285619
bat og here. im heavily underwater with my bat investment at this point but still holding. i'm never going to sell - not because bat will be some kind of currency in the future and i won't need to sell it like some link cope, but I don't want to make profit from BAT anymore and I also don't want anyone to use my tokens for ill purposes. truth be told, after reading that brendan eich in 2006 didn't think that people with xy chromosomes that undergo hrt and srs maybe shouldn't be allowed to compete in female chess tournaments has really had me rethinking my investment and the morality of it. i only want to invest in ethical companies like lockheed martin that actually help make democracy more accessible to global citizens around the world. if over 20 years ago the ceo of brave browser had such a controversial view it makes me concerned that he might have some problematic ideas today. did he even get the covid vaccine? im not sure, and I don't want to find out the hard way. i will simply hold onto my hundreds of thousands of bat, and not sell them, not lend them, just freeze them up on my hard drive in a password i refuse to commit to memory and dispose of my thinkpad into landfill. i encourage all other bat holders with a conscience to do the same.

62286053
>>62286043
Wow. That's some deep pasta bro. Missed this one

62286063
>>62285713
>IT'S BEEN A WHILE
It's been a hot minute. Sup, lad?

>>62285966
>Where are you?
o/
>do you still own BAT?
Of course, lad *sobs *

Haven't seen a thread in a minute. Was planning on doing some thot bait threads when we get a new JanJan.

62286087
>>62286063
>Blue hair
Gross

62286122
>>62286087
I mean if you want me to post men or trannies for you, just give me the word ^_^
_

Anyone else getting some pretty gnarly memory leaks from Brave recently?

62286148
>>62286122
This is better. Thanks. Looks like a boobier version of that Claire Manson fashion bitch on instagram. Thanks TP

62286153
>>62286063
>>62286122
Hawt
Got any uber thicc goths??

62286226
BAT is total shite. You have to be retarded to still believe in this garbage

62286297
>>62285619
if i had invested in LINK instead of BAT i'd be a millionaire right now, and probably retired

62286350
>>62286122
no fucking trannies please.

just hot ass bitches, keep em coming TP, and thanks legend

62286359
>>62286297
If I ate one pile of dogshit instead of another pile of dogshit I'd be puking my brains out harder right now too

62286450
>>62286148
NP. Feel free to drop a request.

>>62286153
Of course!

>>62286297
I absolutely abhor LINK, but same u_u. I literally should've put more money anywhere else.

>>62286350
I'm just fucking around.
>Legend
Much appreciated!

>>62286359
At least STINK pumped. All I wanted was $4 BAT.

62286624
>>62286450
fuark that bitch can get it

62286763
>>62286450
Would happily suffocate devouring her ass while she sits on my face 10/10 slut

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 137 replies — 32.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 137 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:38 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283730
>>62283715
>Either Hedera "servers" interact with them ("step 1")
No the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera.
> or they integrate Hedera's code into their base chain code ("step 2").
It's not "Hedera's code" whatever the fuck that means.

Two ledgers can talk with each others without having to interact with Hedera at all. Cope all you want it's just how it is.

62283741
>>62283730
>the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera
They're Hedera's servers. The servers run Hedera's code.

>It's not "Hedera's code"
CLPR is coded by Hedera. It's Hedera's code.

62283782
>>62283741
Two ledgers can talk with each other via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.

Keep coping jew.

62283798
>>62283782
>via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.
Not talking about the DLT, I'm talking about the project.

62283808
>>62283798
So again, why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level? This makes CCIP and all other bridges obsolete.

62283817
>>62283702
>>62283730
>>62283782
Why do you keep larping about the technicals when they havent been released?

62283819
>>62283808
Firstly because no other chain wants to alter their base code and functionality by injecting someone else's shit.
Secondly because it's retarded to ask blockchain validators/miners/nodes/... to moonlight as oracles.

62283824
>>62283817
Did you even read
https://hashgraph.com/blog/clpr-a-new-bridgeless-standard-for-cross-ledger-communication/
?
I know it's not in depth but it's something

62283873
>>62283824
yeah I screwed up, i meant the guy you were talking to and one other retard. the only rebuttal so far has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible. i think this clpr thing is not getting the serious consideration from CLL it deserves.

62283889
>>62278023
lmao i didnt report shit lol
>>62278047
swapping ids? where faggot? i posted the op in my car, now im on wifi. made that very clear early on.

i get it you guys married your bags, but I didnt and until this is addressed by CLL im going to continue to assume there’s a serious contender until it is addressed by serg.

62283896
>>62283678
you people are so weird constantly id hopping, it gives away that you're jeets, nobody else would care that much on this dead board.
also to the other two faggots, anon engaged you seriously and you gave the game away by getting mad at him, you're not genuine link holders you're hbar baggies spamming the board. you faggots did this once before when link first joined that faggy council trying to act like friends and getting people to buy your bags. hbar is never going to be a thing here, leemon is never going to be a meme, go somewhere else (although i get you're paid to be here so it is what it is)

62283902
>>62283873
What I'm talking about is literally from the documentation.
In all cases, this system requires the introduction of external data from one chain to another.

Step 1 involves "dedicated servers", which basically means third party oracles.

Step 2 involves ledgers implementing CLPR "natively" whereby the validators become the endpoints.
Meaning the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... have to operate as oracles.

62283908
>>62283873
>has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible.
lmao that is exactly what you faggots are doing in this and like 20 other threads on the board right now. meanwhile anon actually rebuts everything and you faggots just pilpul and pretend you didn't hear previous responses.

62283922
>>62283896
>>62283908
>there can be NO questions
keep coping faggot. count my posts and suck these nuts.
>>62283902
ok then post the fucking sauce

62283946
>>62283922
>ok then post the fucking sauce
The sauce is all over this thread

62283951
>>62283922
you are an hbar baggie, you are a faggot, hbar will never ever be a thing

62284227
>>62283896
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62284232
>>62284227
>1pbtid
keep swapping

62284314
>>62283922
It seems like nobody here really knows anything about CLPR. Hell they didn't even know it existed two days ago lol.

62284575
>>62278842
Wow another private key hacked. Chainlink would have prevented this.

62284723
>>62284232
lick my ass, nigger

62284748
>>62284723
>lick my ass, nigger

62285049
>>62283946
>theres no sauce
I get it, this is a curveball, but if we lie to ourselves, like saying Chainlink is the only solution forever and ever, then we sound like xrpcels. the only thing posted as been press releases, nothing more, otherwise i wouldve read that instead of talking to low iq biz retards.
>>62284314
ikr. it’s been almost a month, wish there was more than silence. cll governs hbar they know how clpr works, wish theyd share.
>>62283951
Think what you like faggot. again, suck these hairy nuts and enjoy.

62285504
>>62285049
>>theres no sauce
You were spoonfed the sauce as a url in a direct reply. What is wrong with you.

62285523
>>62285049
>if we lie to ourselves
>we
fuck off

62285569
i'm too demoralized to read this thread. just tell me, are we going to make it?

62285584
>>62285569
CLPR just buckbroke CCIP.
But the rest of Chainlink services are fine.

62285645
>>62285584
Nobody will ever fall for the HBAR shilling, you are so fucking bad at this.

62285660
>>62285645
CLPR doesn't even benefit HBAR
Anyone can use it without using hedera

62286036
>>62285584
It didn't buckbreak anything though. Nothing changes for ccip.
You've been dumpstered several times in this thread and at this point you're clearly just a deranged subhuman repeating his nonsense for the sake of it. This is how your threads usually go when you get your new DOA talking points
>make the OP on your phone
>respond to yourself on your pc for several days
>hop ids on phone to try and back yourself up but it's really obvious while you get schooled
Just kill yourself, fuckin loser lol

item.title: Question for oldfags about vidya prices: — 25 replies — 26.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Question for oldfags about vidya prices:

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-29 00:50 UTC

OP

i looked into the price of SNES games as well as master system back in the days:

-average snes game sold for 50$ in the early 9às, inflation adjusted that's 120$ today
Some sold higher than that : donkey kong came for 70$, so 150$ today

-30 to 60$ for sega master system, but it was the late 80s so like 130$ today.

Why are gooners crying about the price of their vidyas? Things used to be way more expensive for a 2 d platformer that would give you max 100 hours of play if not half. Also today's collector price for most of those games is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new back in the days, so still no profit after holding for 30+ years kek

Early replies (first 3)

62283131
>>62283119
There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.

62283133
>>62283119
Oldfag here, they’re crying because all these games were $1 at your local flea market in 2005, AND THEY DIDN’T EVEN WANT TO BUY. It’s even worse than missing bitcoin because it was right under their nose, they saw, they knew, and they shrugged it off.

62283135
>>62283131
snes games were cheap like 10 + years ago. Master system is still very cheap, but nobody gives a shit. I guess rn the money is in xbox / game cube games

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283136
Also, thread's theme:

https://youtu.be/E5_DDRkH70E?t=458

62284191
So as a former soiboi with a massive collection of everything from
>atari
>nes
>snes
>genesis/32x
>n64
>gc
>ps1
>dc
What am I in for and also when/how do I sell to get max value for all this shit that tbqhfam I'm just as comfy emulating? Good crt monitors are like digging for dinosaur bones and composite input screens are like asking about where the bluray players are these days. This shit is collecting dust and I've got projects that need funding

62284329
>>62283119
Back then the only markets were NA, SA, EU and JP, and there were fewer people in those markets. Also it was mostly for kids, while now it's for kids + teens + adults + grandmas, and both genders. Also now China and India have entered the market, so there are a lot more consumers. This allows developers to split the software cost on a lot more consumers, and the production of the hardware became cheaper as well, and online digital distribution basically killed cartridges and discs. The cartridges used to cost a lot.

62284510
>>62284329
>Back then the only markets were NA, SA, EU and JP
We didn't know how good we had it. Aryan markets only. No surprise it all went downhill.

>>62284191
well, some retards actually bought the old game to play them, and the price dropped when they were reedited, like final fantasy 7, got myself a copy for 30$ after the remake. Before that it was 100$.
But, as an amateur of fine things, i have to say there is something unique about playing on the exact hardware of the time.

I don't expect that much of a growth for retro video games because of that. Also the hardware self destruct, you have to be able repair and maitain it and some chips can't be bought back.
However, some stuff is rare and will get only get more scarce so it could increase exponentially this decade.

62284586
You people are so fucking stupid. Its the cartridge. Compared to the 20th century memory and manufacturing is dirt cheap.
People don't want 80$ video games because its genuinely ripping them off. Add in that a majority of game sales are digital now and therefore have lower overhead for distribution costs, and you're really paying that extra 20$ for DEI consulting, infinity third world outsourcing for cutscenes, and to pay one jillion middle managers salaries.
Bundle that with a corporate structure that milks people dry with microtransactions and treating customers like "consumers" and people won't fucking buy.

62284597
>>62284586
nigger, i played 1K hours of counter strike and bought the game 10 years ago for 8 eurobuxxes. Do you think you could get the same value from a 130$ 2 d platformers finished in 30 hours?

62284605
>>62284597
Thats the final factor. Collectible value. Physical copies are tangible and worth their value in resale.

62284628
>>62284605
agreed. But i still find that those snes games and other old gen platforms were extremely expensive. Obviously at the time it was the best video games available so people were happy to pay but that's still very expensive, especially if you play some sega master system who aged so poorly you can't even be bother to play them 2 hours. 130++$ for a game like that was super expensive.

62284656
>>62284628
I just explained it you dumb faggot. READ

62284666
>>62284656
you didn't explain shit, it was very expensive, a bunch of plastic and one mass produced circuit board isn't worth that much

62284681
>>62284666
0 iq mongrel. Kys

62284717
>>62284681
same fagging phone poster, opinion discarded

62284778
>>62283119
I think you'd just rent most of your games from a video store and then get like one or two for Christmas. Also people would share games with their friends because they weren't socially-handicapped tiktok scrollers.

62285855
>>62284778
rent games? so lame. did anyone actually do that? you buy and resell second hand, game cost you 0 basically that's what i used to do with my ps2

62286204
>>62284628
>at the time it was the best video games available so people
Home video games sucked compared to arcade games and everybody knew it, unless you had a Neo Geo (but each game was like $700 in today’s money)

62286216
>>62286204
Street Fighter II for SNES was amazing despite not being arcade where some nignog could chimp out after getting assblasted by Blanka

62286230
>>62285855
>rent games? so lame.
Less so than selling your own games.

>did anyone actually do that?
Yes and it was amazing, especially when renting those esoteric JP games and playing them with an adapter. It was like temporarily unlocking a new world, you were inviting all your friends to explore these games to the fullest for the weekend.

Of course a lonely consoomer from 2026 can’t get it.

62286236
>>62286216
It was very enjoyable indeed, but still not as good as the arcade version.

62286393
>>62283119
My most sacred memory is when my mom picked me up from 1st grade in 1987 and as we are driving home she told me that she got me a new video game where you can SAVE the game and start from there when you come back to it. WOW.

62286541
>>62283119
When I got my first PC in 1997 at 14 I rarely bought games after that. I had consoles but I didn't have money to buy games and my parents wouldn't just buy me any game I wanted whenever.

62286565
>>62283119
They're bitching just to bitch. There's also like 1000 ways to emulate those games and play them like the originals or better, so really they're retardedly nostalgic to have THE cartridge and then mad a bunch of other people are too

62286669
>>62283133
Nobody is talking about used video games. They're crying because modern consoles are approaching a thousand bucks and moden games are heading towards 100. But anon correctly noted that back in the day, it was $50-60 for a new NES cartridge or a new computer game on three 360kb diskettes, and at the time minimum wage was something like $4 an hour. Adjusted for inflation, a game like Super Mario that you could beat in an hour, or Kings Quest that lasts you a couple of hours max, cost $161.
